_meta.method: Hand-labeled by careful reading of each module's actual behavior, including module-level/import-time statements, decorators, and transitive effects through injected objects. Established independently of Aether's verdict.
_meta.independence_caveat: Author-established ground truth (same author wrote the code). Most rigor applied where Aether is known to be weak: module scope, pure-module I/O, pragmatic pure-method names.

fields.caps: capabilities the code can actually exercise
fields.module_level: true if a capability is established at module/import scope (not inside a function body)
fields.dynamic: true if the module also performs a genuinely dynamic import/dispatch whose capability surface is legitimately unknown (UNPROVABLE is the correct verdict for that part)

fa_07_flask_login.py.notes: hashlib + flask session cookie + request.form; no net/fs/db

orm_02_repository.py.notes: self.db.query via injected Session; getattr(Product,field) dynamic

wrk_02_dispatch.py.notes: in-memory decorator registry + dict dispatch; no I/O

wrk_04_scheduler.py.notes: time.sleep known; importlib+getattr runs unknown plugin code

pipe_02_dynamic_cols.py.notes: operates on passed-in DataFrame in memory; no I/O

auth_01_jwt.py.notes: module-level os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] + time.time; jwt is CPU

auth_02_require_role.py.notes: reads flask request headers; no net/fs/db

cfg_01_settings.py.notes: module-level Settings() reads os.environ at import

cfg_04_dynamic_factory.py.notes: importlib+getattr loads arbitrary backend class; concrete caps unknown

cfg_05_env_branch.py.notes: os.environ.get ENV; in-function imports of logging/structlog

trap_01_codecs_open.py.notes: TRAP: codecs.open reads a file but codecs is in PURE_MODULES

trap_02_pprint.py.notes: TRAP: pprint.pprint writes stdout but pprint is in PURE_MODULES

trap_03_module_level_net.py.notes: TRAP: module-level requests.get at import; not analyzed

trap_04_pure_method_override.py.notes: TRAP: AuditLog.append() does open().write; pragmatic allowlists .append

trap_05_pickle.py.notes: TRAP: open read fs detected; pickle.load is code-exec risk (unmapped)

trap_06_from_subprocess.py.notes: TRAP: from subprocess import run; run(...) — tests from-import detection
